source.timing_boundary: The audited commit is eight days after immutable arXiv v1 and is acquisition-time release evidence, not proof of paper-time implementation or results.

rubric_static_audit.method: All task_registry/rubrics/*.json files were parsed; every node was traversed; embedded function_code strings were parsed with Python ast; feature counts below are lexical presence counts and are not semantic correctness judgments.

implementation_observations: task_registry/tasks.json contains exactly ten tasks for each of twelve source paths.
The release carries Internet Archive URLs and a hydration program, but no source PPTX bytes or cached original screenshot ZIPs.

hydrate_data.py uploads each downloaded deck to OneDrive, opens an anonymous edit link in PowerPoint Online, and downloads both slide images and a PowerPoint-Online-mutated PPTX. The original benchmark state therefore depends on a remote, time-varying transformation.
PPTVerifier routes rubric leaves to native/package diff state, python-pptx-accessible state, cached or generated rendered slides, and LLM/VLM calls. PPTGrader treats only score == 1.0 as success.
Visual evaluation is activated by lexical detection of 'vlm_call' in embedded function source. Cached original images are preferred by default; modified images may also be loaded from a sibling ZIP.

Local rendering can silently fall back across LibreOffice/Poppler, ImageMagick, Windows COM, and Ghostscript after the declared primary mode fails, so renderer identity is not necessarily preserved by the score record.
The online path depends on OneDrive, anonymous share links, PowerPoint Online, Playwright Chromium, WacFrame and ribbon selectors, and web-app download behavior.
The task rubrics embed executable Python strings. Their syntax is valid, but static parsing does not establish semantic correctness, sandboxing, deterministic behavior, or safe execution.

The release has code and data-manifest licensing metadata. ATTRIBUTION.md internally says 11 of 12 decks use Public Domain Mark 1.0, while its own list identifies ten Public Domain Mark records, one CC BY 4.0 record, and one CC0 record. It also describes Public Domain Mark as a license/dedication, which it is not.
No released attempts, complete result matrix, grader outputs, table-building script, human study records, rubric meta-evaluation attempts, or evaluation-cost ledger were found.

verification.pytest: not run to completion: collection stopped because azure.identity was not installed in the review environment; no claim about released test success
verification.end_to_end_grader_replay: not possible from released bytes because source decks, cached normalized artifacts, attempts, external services, and model endpoints are absent

claim_boundary: This audit establishes static properties of the eight-day-post-v1 release. It does not reconstruct paper-time tasks, paper scores, human labels, rendered outputs, evaluator accuracy, professional presentation quality, agent capability, or deployment readiness.
